allowed.title: Next.js HTTP request deserialization can lead to DoS when using insecure React Server Components
allowed.reason: Next 15+ migration is blocked by the UI lock until app/layout dynamic client-only initializers are moved without visual changes.

allowed.title: Next.js has a Denial of Service with Server Components
allowed.reason: Tracked as framework migration debt; no new UI-surface changes may be bundled into dependency-only hardening.

allowed.title: Next.js Vulnerable to Denial of Service with Server Components
allowed.reason: Tracked as framework migration debt; upgrade requires a dedicated Next 15/16 compatibility PR.

allowed.title: Next.js vulnerable to server-side request forgery in applications using WebSocket upgrades
allowed.reason: Tracked as framework migration debt; this exception prevents unrelated new advisories from being masked.

allowed.title: Next.js has a Middleware / Proxy bypass in Pages Router applications using i18n
allowed.reason: Tracked as framework migration debt pending a zero-UI-change Next major migration.

allowed.title: sharp inherited vulnerabilities in libvips: CVE-2026-33327, CVE-2026-33328, CVE-2026-35590, CVE-2026-35591
allowed.reason: Sharp 0.35 is a breaking native-image upgrade and Next.js plus Transformers currently resolve 0.34.x; keep this time-bounded exception visible until a dedicated compatibility upgrade is validated.

allowed.title: brace-expansion: DoS via unbounded expansion length causing an out-of-memory process crash
allowed.reason: Upstream published no backport for the 1.x/2.x maintenance lines (1.1.16 and 2.1.2 are the newest releases there and remain in range). The only patched release, 5.0.8, changed the CommonJS export from a callable function to an object, so every minimatch@3/5/9 on the remaining paths would throw; the 5.x nodes are already forced to 5.0.8 via overrides. Residual paths are exceljs>archiver@5 (internal XLSX zip paths, no user-controlled globs) and the license-checker tooling.
